findings.title: Core artifact contract is mixed with adapter-specific state
findings.detail: The proposal puts durable change artifacts, gitignored per-run state, and review-ledger backup behavior under one `ArtifactStore` without defining whether they share the same namespace, lifecycle, and portability guarantees. That makes the abstraction boundary itself ambiguous and risks leaking local-filesystem concerns into core design. Revise the proposal to separate or explicitly define these storage domains and state which behaviors are core requirements versus `LocalFsArtifactStore` implementation details.

findings.title: Artifact-phase gate behavior is not specific enough to design
findings.detail: The proposal says phase requirements will become an explicit gate matrix, but it does not say whether the matrix must preserve current behavior or intentionally change validation, nor how legacy or partially populated changes/runs should be treated. Design needs the authoritative workflow behavior up front. Add acceptance criteria for required artifacts per phase, transition failure behavior, and compatibility expectations for existing data.

findings.title: External-runtime goal lacks backend-agnostic constraints
findings.detail: The motivation is to enable future external runtimes, but the proposal only names a filesystem-backed implementation and a minimal `read/write/list/exists` API. That is not enough to guide a backend-neutral design. Capture the required invariants now, such as artifact identity model, payload expectations, atomic update needs, and any consistency or concurrency guarantees the abstraction must preserve across adapters.

findings.title: Run-domain artifact model is incomplete and internally inconsistent
findings.detail: The proposal declares two run artifacts (`run-state` and `run history`), but the invariant section, capability text, and acceptance criteria only model `run-state`, and Acceptance Criterion 3 still describes the universal identity key as `(changeId, artifactType, qualifier?)`. Clarify whether run history is a first-class artifact in scope for this change, and define a run-domain identity contract that consistently covers `runId`-scoped artifacts so the design does not have to invent conflicting store shapes.

REQUEST_CHANGES||R3-F04|proposal.md|consistency|medium|Run-domain artifact model is incomplete and internally inconsistent
